response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior generalist with genuine systems experience (low-latency D services handling millions of requests/second, Kubernetes-deployed platforms) and evident mentoring inclination through workshops and courses. However, he clearly misses the core minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ — his C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project — and his large-scale infrastructure and cloud architecture experience is fragmented rather than sustained. He also lacks a formal technical-leadership role owning platform strategy, making him a poor fit for this specific C++-centric compute-platform lead position despite solid engineering credentials.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Broad full-stack and DevOps range with strong testing/TDD craftsmanship culture
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing via workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ requirement (~7 months professional C++)
No formal technical-lead role managing priorities, deadlines, or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers
Cloud/infrastructure experience is intermittent and product-focused, not sustained large-scale platform ownership
